correct_answer: X-rays of the left hand and wrist

highlights.text: The most appropriate next step in diagnosis is a bone age x-ray to evaluate skeletal maturation
bone age x-ray is the initial test of choice because it can help differentiate constitutional delay of growth and puberty from other etiologies of short stature and delayed puberty.
